channel.title: Daily Summary on TzJ's Net
channel.description: Recent content in Daily Summary on TzJ's Net

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-19
item.description: Set up website build environment on Linux, debugged translation pipeline failures, and fixed cross-platform compatibility issues

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-18
item.description: Continued authentication issues have prevented any work from being carried out that day.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-17
item.description: Created task-switching checkpoint skill, enhanced MeetingHelper with multi-language ASR, Metal GPU acceleration, and real-time noise suppression, then conducted comprehensive website code review fixing 11 bugs including critical CSS layout issue

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-16
item.description: The error recovery demonstration system has been refactored, the verification code library has been simplified, and the quota rules have been relaxed. Additionally, a proxy skill plugin has been installed globally for the Claude code.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-15
item.description: Systematically debugged and resolved ErrorRecovery robotics project environment setup, fixing 5 critical bugs blocking Coffee task execution, then established full reproducibility by tracking modified dependencies and creating automated patch system for cluster deployment

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-14
item.description: The development of 5 core MIHD modules has been completed (including HD dataset support, label mixing analysis, ARI stability testing, patch size experiments, and neighborhood query integration). The 18GB checkpoint data has been uploaded to the athena server. Additionally, the git conflict issue in the TokenMonitor project has been resolved, and the error recovery benchmark testing process and data collection workflow have been implemented. Furthermore, 6 successful task case videos have been produced. An independent Claude code environment has been configured on the Tianhe server, and issues related to the gadget rclone configuration have been fixed.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-13
item.description: All four projects have achieved significant milestones: the draft manuscript for the MIHD paper has been completed, along with the development of multimodal fusion diagnosis technology. The MeetingHelper dual-audio transcription system has been upgraded. The architecture for error recovery benchmark tests has been established, and the outline of the NeurIPS report has been finalized. The bugs in the BOSS LeRobot process have also been fixed. Additionally, progress has been made in the research on basic models related to batteries.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-12
item.description: Advanced research process development in two projects: planning the MIHD spatial transcriptomics benchmark test, as well as documentation and troubleshooting related to error recovery.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-11
item.description: High-intensity technical workdays across four devices: completing the BetterSSH full-stack project from scratch to production, optimizing the multi-project development toolchain, deeply refactoring the MimicGen augmentation system, building a NeurIPS academic writing framework, and expanding the speech recognition backend.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-10
item.description: Multi-project development day: Completed the writing of the MIHD benchmark paper, resolved the compatibility issues between MeetingHelper and Screenpipe, debugged the LIBERO and battery training processes, implemented the OpenClaw functionality, and avoided technical debt issues. Additionally, improved the pricing accuracy of TokenMonitor to better meet Claude’s needs.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-09
item.description: Cross-device full-stack development day: Optimized the robot learning process on the Tianhe platform, including MimicGen analysis, human data collection workflows, and multi-dataset contrast learning. Addressed infrastructure issues such as Node proxies and permission handling for Claude code. Reorganized key components and implemented Chinese-to-English translation functionality through Ollama. Also completed two major architecture upgrades, such as integrating LifeCopilot with OpenClaw and adding 70 new test scenarios. Developed meeting assistance features from scratch using Screenpipe and MCP. Additionally, improved TokenMonitor by adding timezone-aware SSH processing and user interface animation optimization.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-08
item.description: Automated processing of cross-platform code optimization and translation functions has been completed, involving multiple high-performance computing/robotics projects. Three machine learning data pipelines have been debugged, and the translation infrastructure has been migrated to the local Ollama platform. Additionally, the robot enhancement system has been redesigned, with the action replay mode converted to target pose execution mode.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-07
item.description: Fixed 4 bugs in the website/update.sh script. Set up a complete Hugo deployment environment. The website update process has recovered from complete failure to pass all 7 steps. At the same time, started the full daily/weekly report MCP synchronization process.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-06
item.description: Planned and executed three edits to the MIHD paper draft, including rewriting Intro P1, expanding Abstract, and R5/R6. Additionally, three new cross-slice query experiments were run, and the HD Niche Query visualization was fixed.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-05
item.description: We have completed the refactoring of the Linux FloatBall fixed window + GDK Input Shape architecture. This refactoring was aimed at completely addressing the issue of geometric offsets at the platform level. Additionally, we systematically resolved two major problems: the absence of structured logs in the Life-Copilot Discord Bot, and permission errors encountered by the MCP tool.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-04
item.description: Deeply debugged and fixed multiple system-level bugs of TokenMonitor FloatBall on Linux (fundamental issues with GTK size negotiation, WM clamp positioning offset, etc.). Completed a systematic research study for the paper presented at NeurIPS 2025 D&B Track 63. Also implemented several major features of Life-Copilot (eliminated 2,823 lines in the Discord Bot architecture, fully implemented Screen Time Tracker, rapid idea capture for the WeChat Bot, backend integration with Codex/Anthropic). Additionally, explored the feasibility of remote SSH access via AionUI.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-03
item.description: The MIHD project has completed additional experiments on HD fusion, organized the narrative lines of the paper, and started writing the bilingual skeleton. We have established a distinct research focus on zero-shot FM embedding fusion. TokenMonitor has been updated to fix an error in Rust compilation, and the bug fixing for the Linux GTK window positioning issue continues. Gadget has completed two rounds of rclone bidirectional synchronization and investigated the destination of NIPS research data.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-02
item.description: Dual-device collaboration: DCC is responsible for building the experimental framework for the MIHD paper (using GPU Slurm scripts and three visualization scripts). DesktopLinux will fix the platform bugs of TokenMonitor in Linux/Wayland, as well as address the 21 code review issues mentioned in PR #4. These issues have been uploaded to GitHub. Additionally, DesktopLinux will initialize the new machine’s environment and configure the Claude Code development tools.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-04-01
item.description: The DCC side completed a comprehensive comparative experiment with Leiden data (the core conclusion: high-quality embedding methods are superior to clustering methods), and created a visual representation of queries in the HD domain. The DesktopLinux side completed the initialization of the new Linux workstation system, as well as the construction of TokenMonitor Tauri. Additionally, through two rounds of iterative optimization, issues such as unstable positions of floating buttons on Linux systems and display problems with residual shadows after closing were resolved across different platforms.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-31
item.description: I have completed the weekly work plan for the MIHD paper: Leiden clustering integration, STHD Ground Truth integration. I have also successfully run the DLPFC Leiden baseline and HD full-process evaluation, obtaining key experimental data.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-30
item.description: The embedding extraction and multi-strategy fusion testing of the TEDDY-G 400M gene base model on all 11 slices of the DLPFC have been completed. A W13-week report has been written, summarizing the experimental results from last week.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-29
item.description: Analyzed the TEDDY single-cell basic model paper on the HPC cluster, fixed 4 critical bugs in the existing extraction script, and successfully completed the embedding extraction deployment for the DLPFC dataset.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-28
item.description: 提升cchypothesis的调试能力，实现智能双轨排查机制；修复TokenMonitor仪表板中的抖动问题以及窗口定位错误。

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-27
item.description: A solution based on hypothesis debugging skills has been created, and this skill was successfully utilized to solve the complex issue of the TokenMonitor interface flickering.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-26
item.description: Through five major iterations, the ccplan skills were systematically improved (including prompt calibration, multi-intention decomposition, feature protection protocols, etc.). The TokenMonitor was completely reimplemented, with improvements such as file division, type safety, and caching optimization. The float ball interface was developed and continuously refined across more than eight conversations. The cost calculation function was moved to the ccusage command-line tool. Relevant architectural documents were also provided for contributors.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-25
item.description: Architecture improvements: gadget project module reorganization, skill ecosystem optimization, TokenMonitor cross-platform adaptation

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-24
item.description: This module has been reorganized into a structure consisting of 8 components, with 72 test scenarios. Additionally, through the use of paper-based documentation, three Claude code skills have been designed. Furthermore, comprehensive cross-platform migration plans have been developed for TokenMonitor. More than 350 lines of code that were only applicable to macOS have been removed.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-23
item.description: The environment initialization was completed in the DCC HPC cluster (using rclone for data synchronization, checking the status of GPU nodes, installing relevant software). At the same time, the issues present in the MIHD research project were identified and documented in a document.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-22
item.description: The batch generation of historical reports for W05-W12 has been completed. Vulnerabilities in the deployment of key websites have been fixed (such as updates to the PaperMod theme and improvements to the robustness of the update.sh script). Additionally, the Hugo deployment architecture for Gadgets has been reengineered, resulting in a unified output/pre-release area for sites, a dropdown navigation function for bugJournal, and a mechanism for publishing benchmark test reports.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-21
item.description: The development environment is now ready. The critical errors in the abstract generation process have been fixed. Support for Codex has been implemented, and all AI agents have been upgraded to Opus 4.6.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-20
item.description: This project was run on a MacBook equipped with small tools: daily reports for the period ending 2026-03-19 were generated in bulk, all documents were fully updated, and code verification was performed (7 errors were fixed). A vulnerability in rclone sync-all was identified and fixed (this resolved the issue of repeated calls to the LLM due to the lack of reports). A complete Git history cleanup was performed, and 452 personal files were removed using the rclone synchronization tool. The total size of these files was 94MB. As a result, the repository size was reduced from 22MB to 516KB.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-19
item.description: Cross-device multi-project research and development were conducted: spatial transcriptomics alignment experiments revealed the trade-offs associated with semantic batch mixing; a robot error recovery process, from training optimization to portable deployment, has been validated through 139 tests; CalendarPro/Life Copilot completed architectural restructuring, eliminating the bottleneck caused by single intent classifiers; at the same time, knowledge management infrastructure was expanded, including tools for the improved 5-mode system, capable of handling long-text analysis tasks; Yuke Zhu analyzed his academic trajectory from 2012 to 2026, publishing over 100 papers and conducting more than 10 analysis iterations; additionally, 30 days of historical data from February to March 2026 were processed in batches.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-18
item.description: The CalendarPro has added a batch update feature, which allows handling of multi-task status reports. Currently, there is complete test coverage, and the relevant documentation has also been updated.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-17
item.description: Cross-device collaboration has been implemented to make progress in MIHD space science reconstruction; the potential bridging mechanisms of QCVLA have been studied in depth, and an improved approach inspired by FLARE has been adopted; issues related to the VLA training/inferencing infrastructure have been resolved; data for error-based benchmarking have been generated; and the performance of system tools has also been improved.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-16
item.description: 已经完成了对空间转录组学相关基础模型的交叉嵌入诊断工作；设计并实现了一个错误恢复流程，使得数据量减少了81%；修复了robosuite坐标方面的关键漏洞；开始大规模场景生成的工作；与30多位研究人员合作，开发了一套学术分析工具。

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-15
item.description: The comprehensive optimization of the CalendarPro system has been completed. A 7-stage parallel processing scheme was adopted, and the system successfully passed 230 tests. Additionally, a unified small tool development toolkit was implemented, integrating paper retrieval, performance analysis, and citation information into a single command-line interface. The shared tools were also extracted and placed in a public package, thereby avoiding the need to write more than 400 lines of duplicate code. All output directories have also been reorganized into a structured hierarchical structure. Furthermore, performance analyses were conducted on 13 leading researchers in robotics and artificial intelligence, enabling systematic tracking of their academic research progress.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-14
item.description: The script for GPU monitoring features an adaptive UI, a HDF5 data visualization tool, as well as four sets of progress prediction experimental frameworks for the pi05 model.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-13
item.description: This academic researcher profiling tool utilizes large language models for trajectory analysis and includes a reward recognition function. Additionally, it implements a tracking system for the progress of each operation in every frame during the RoboTwin Place dual shoe task.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-12
item.description: Fixed the sandbox permissions structure in the macOS Wallpaper app, and resolved issues related to PyTorch dependencies and CUDA compilation for deep learning evaluation environments.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-11
item.description: Multi-project productivity improvement: refactoring MIHD files and performance evaluation, development of K8s GPU monitoring tools, optimization of VLA training schemes (through improvements to the torch/torchvision library and leveraging JAX technology to optimize dependencies, resulting in a 33% speed increase).

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-10
item.description: 在多个项目开发日中，我们进行的任务包括：文档整理、空间转录组学的调试、机器人学习的优化，以及多个代码库之间出现的环境问题的排查。这些工作涉及到超过6个不同的代码框架。

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-09
item.description: Completed the core functions of MIHD spatial transcriptomics (cross-sample RM-IDEAL evaluation, CrossModalEnhancer cross-modal enhancement, scGPT literature research), advanced multiple VLA/robot projects (UniVLA document processing, RoboTwin data conversion, process matching analysis), implemented the server-based deployment of the gadget tool MCP and its integration with CalendarPro, and enhanced the support capabilities of research_scout for multiple data sources.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-08
item.description: 扩展的研究工具，包括作者搜索功能以及更新的文档资料；能够生成错误情况的可视化展示；设计并实现了用于VLA训练的关键区域标注系统；同时还修复了RoboTwin的渲染基础设施问题。

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-07
item.description: Four devices are running simultaneously: development of the MIHD documentation system and research related to scGPT has been completed; the FLARE/Phoenix training module has been launched, and four evaluation issues related to BC-RNN have been fixed; the WKWebView has been used to implement the function of desktop video web page background images; historical reports for February over the past 20 days have been generated in bulk; the context-aware intent classification function in the CalendarPro tool has been completed; moreover, a complete and feature-rich research paper management system has been built from scratch, including language configuration, reverse initialization, and deduplication functions.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-06
item.description: The cross-project collaboration includes data analysis using spatial transcriptomics, repairs to the CalendarPro software, and improvement of related documentation. Additionally, it involves refactoring the robot learning code library, optimizing the Docker rendering stack, and designing the Phoenix/FLARE training process.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-05
item.description: Cross-device parallel development, covering the MIHD spatial transcriptomics domain (through RM-IDEAL cross-sample benchmark tests, revealing performance characteristics at different levels); improvements to the device toolkit (with monthly summary updates and a complete guide to Claude code); debugging of the VLA/Openpi-moe training process (fixing issues during curobo installation and errors in standardization); and comprehensive upgrade of the CalendarPro personal service system (adding 16 new features, achieving 31 goals, while conducting a system code audit to identify critical vulnerabilities and potential dangerous handling mechanisms).

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-04
item.description: Multi-project research and documentation day: Studied the cross-sample query evaluation method used in the QueST paper for spatial transcriptomics data; improved the Desktop Video CLAUDE.md tool; wrote a comprehensive training guide for the zhaoganlong self-reflection framework, including detailed descriptions of each checkpoint; simultaneously updated the error_recovery_benchmark document to align it with the actual code implementation, including a redesigned process architecture.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-03
item.description: The MIHD cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark testing has been completed. This includes the design of the script, which consists of 781 lines of code. We also addressed various vulnerabilities and performed visual processing tasks. There are also 459 lines of documentation accompanying this work. On the DCC platform, we processed historical AI conversation logs over a period of 13 days and successfully converted these logs into structured daily reports. Additionally, we developed the BC-RNN/Pi0.5 evaluation framework. However, we encountered some critical configuration errors during the testing on the Tianhe platform. Finally, we improved the user interface of the screen saver and prepared the necessary website documentation.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-02
item.description: We have corrected several critical errors in the MIHD benchmark tests, which were caused by the limitations of the STAIG architecture. Additionally, we have resolved any permission-related issues associated with macOS sandboxes, using a security domain-based tagging mechanism to handle these problems. We have conducted extensive training on the GPU cluster using the Pi0.5 VLA dataset, and provided a detailed list of external dependencies required for the project. Furthermore, we identified potential bottlenecks in the GPU usage during the evaluation process.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-01
item.description: Cross-device work tasks have been completed: the integration of the MIHD high-quality dataset has been achieved, and multi-source annotations have been verified. A large-scale refactoring of the code library has been carried out, resulting in the cleanup of over 250,000 lines of code. Twenty-eight historical daily reports have been generated, and the workflow has been optimized. Additionally, issues with the BC-RNN/Pi0.5 training process have been fixed, enabling simultaneous training on six GPUs. Finally, Version 4.1 of Desktop Video has been released, with fixes for playback problems and a redesigned interface for the Liquid Glass module.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-28
item.description: An advanced spatial transcriptomics analysis pipeline, supporting Visium HD technology, and equipped with comprehensive RM-IDEAL benchmark capabilities. Key issues related to the scGPT model loading have been resolved, resulting in a 44% improvement in clustering metrics. The desktop wallpaper function “History” has also been implemented, and problems with power management under multiple screens have been addressed. Additionally, a VLA evaluation process has been established, revealing that despite being trained on target tasks, the Phoenix model still performs poorly.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-27
item.description: The Slurm cluster infrastructure was studied, the storage usage in the shared file system was analyzed, and the evaluation results of the machine learning models were reviewed, in order to understand their performance in error recovery.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-26
item.description: These tasks involve work on various devices: creating LaTeX-resumes on a MacBook; building a comprehensive BC-RNN image pattern training system with a multi-task evaluation framework; and exploring the evaluation infrastructure for the Pi0.5 model on the Tianhe platform.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-23
item.description: Through vectorization and GPU batch processing techniques, we have successfully optimized the MIHD fusion process, resulting in a 100 to 500 times improvement in running speed. Additionally, we improved the STAIG algorithm to support various visual encoding methods. We presented comprehensive results of visual optimization experiments in 11 slides. We also fixed the crash issue in the error recovery benchmark test and restarted the training process for Pi0.5 Phoenix. Furthermore, we improved the monitoring mechanism.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-22
item.description: The two-stage fusion implementation of MIHD visual refinement has been completed, and preliminary testing and comprehensive file cleanup have been carried out. The related documentation has also been updated, and batch historical reports have been generated. Additionally, the second stage evaluation of the error recovery benchmark test has been performed, using GPU scheduling optimization and a Pi0.5 fine-tuning process setup.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-21
item.description: Scripts for evaluating the pi0.5 and mimicgen baseline models have been created, but subsequent operations were not completed due to issues with cluster GPU access permissions.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-20
item.description: In-depth infrastructure construction is underway in two projects: a spatial transcriptomics caching system and a robotic technology error recovery data generation process.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-19
item.description: There are two important research projects: first, the comprehensive documentation of MIHD spatial transcriptomics data; second, analyzing the fundamental limitations of this visual encoder. In addition, we have completed the second phase of the error recovery benchmark test and formulated corresponding implementation plans.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-18
item.description: We have fixed the critical spatial coordinate errors in the MIHD multimodal fusion benchmark test, and we have also established the infrastructure for robot policy visualization and checkpoint management.

item.title: Defect record: 2026-02-17
item.description: Daily AI Dialogue Summary:

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-16
item.description: The integration process for VLA with the error recovery benchmark tests (versions 4.3 to 4.5) has been completed. We have diagnosed and analyzed the crash issues in the MIHD clustering structure, and designed functionality for the dialogue summary module as mentioned in the daily reports.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-15
item.description: Multi-project development day: Publish daily reports on the Hugo website. Monitor long-term MIHD benchmark experiments on the DCC cluster. Complete the refactoring of the CalendarPro code library and add a scheduled task function. Improved the Gadget/Summarize tools to support atomic writes and multi-device synchronization capabilities. Developed an advanced error recovery benchmark scheme, which includes 118 scenarios and a policy-based generation framework.

item.title: Defect record: 2026-02-14
item.description: Summary of daily artificial intelligence conversations

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-13
item.description: Daily summary of artificial intelligence discussions

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-12
item.description: We have improved the documentation for MIHD, and developed a detailed seven-stage optimization plan. This plan includes normalization techniques, Q-Former fusion methods, and feature design inspired by QueST.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-10
item.description: The completed spatial transcriptomics benchmark test utilized a combination of PCA, UNI, and STAIG techniques. It was applied to 11 tissue sections, resulting in a total of 7 successful runs.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-09
item.description: The visualization of the MIHD benchmark has been enhanced, with various metrics displayed. Additionally, a comprehensive RM-Ideal evaluation of the DLPFC region has been initiated.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-08
item.description: The RM-Ideal spatial niche matching evaluation algorithm has been implemented and integrated into the QueST and MIHD frameworks. This allows for realistic testing of the quality of embeddings.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-07
item.description: Daily AI Dialogue Summary

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-06
item.description: The process for submitting public benchmark results has been implemented. This process is deployed using GitHub Pages, and includes an interactive mechanism for user consent.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-05
item.description: The end-to-end GCN+STAIG fusion system has been implemented and debugged. Six critical issues were identified and fixed (including four algorithm alignment problems and two flexibility-related errors). Additionally, a hyperparameter tuning issue was detected, which resulted in a performance degradation of nine times.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-03
item.description: 我们已经修复了关于MIHD的基准测试文档不完整的问题，通过生成新的可视化结果并更新实验日志来完善相关内容。

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-02
item.description: We have studied the benchmark results of MIHD and identified any missing relevant documentation.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-01
item.description: The problem of performance decline in the MIHD spatial transcriptomics model has been fixed (performance decreased from 0.4 to 0.13). The STAIG GCN alignment algorithm has been implemented. It has also been determined that environmental conditions and clustering methods are key factors affecting model performance.

item.title: Error log: 2025-10-02
item.description: In the AutoSeg-SAM2 project, open the relevant source files. Currently, the session has not actually begun.
